analogy: Think of "Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate" through the chapter's own opening images: Transportation in Animals and Plants 7 Y ou have learnt earlier that all organisms need food, water and oxygen for survival. They need to transport all these to various parts of their body. Further, animals need to transport wastes to parts from where they can... The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: 7.1 CIRCULATORY S YSTEM, TRANSPORTATION IN ANIMALS AND PLANTS 71, SCIENCE 72, TRANSPORTATION IN ANIMALS AND PLANTS 73. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that transportation in Animals and Plants 7 Y ou have learnt earlier that all organisms need food, water and oxygen for survival.

The text explains that they need to transport all these to various parts of their body.

The text explains that further, animals need to transport wastes to parts from where they can be removed.

The text explains that they function to transport substances and together form the circulatory system.

The text explains that in this chapter, you shall learn about transport of substances in animals and plants.

The text explains that 7.1 CIRCULATORY S YSTEM Blood What happens when you get a cut on your body?

The text explains that blood is the fluid which flows in blood vessels.

The text explains that it transports substances like digested food from the small intestine to the other parts of the body.

The text explains that it carries oxygen from the lungs to the cells of the body.

The text explains that it also transports waste for removal from the body.

The text explains that blood is composed of a fluid, called plasma in which different types of cells are suspended.

The text explains that heart Vein Artery Why is the colour of blood red ?

The text explains that 7.1 Circulatory system (Arteries are shown in red colour and veins in blue)

The text explains that tRANSPORTATION IN ANIMALS AND PLANTS 71 One type of cells are the red blood cells (RBC) which contain a red pigment called haemoglobin.

The text explains that haemoglobin binds with oxygen and transports it to all the parts of the body and ultimately to all the cells.

The text explains that it will be difficult to provide oxygen efficiently to all the cells of the body without haemoglobin.

The text explains that this blood has to go back to the heart for transport to the lungs for removal of carbon dioxide as you have learnt in Chapter 6.

The text explains that since the blood flow is rapid and at a high pressure, the arteries have thick elastic walls.

The text explains that paheli explained that the pulmonary artery carries blood from the heart, so it is called an artery and not a vein.

The text explains that blood can be donated at hospitals and other places authorised by the government.

The text explains that 7.4 Sections of human heart Vena Cava Right atrium Right ventricle Partition completely separating the two halves Left atrium Left ventricle Pulmonary artery Pulmonary vein mixing up of blood rich in oxygen with the blood rich in carbon dioxide.

The text explains that to understand the functioning of the circulatory system, start from the right side of the heart in Fig.

The text explains that these arrows show the direction of the blood flow from the heart Aorta

The text explains that animals such as sponges and Hydra do not possess any circulatory system.

The text explains that the process of removal of wastes produced in the cells of the living organisms is called excretion.

The text explains that the parts involved in excretion form the excretory system.

The text explains that excretory system in humans The waste which is present in the blood has to be removed from the body.

The text explains that this process is called dialysis. tube-like ureters.

The text explains that the kindeys, ureters, bladder and urethra form the excretory system.

The text explains that aquatic animals like fishes, excrete cell waste as ammonia which directly dissolves in water.

The text explains that 7.6 Human excretory system Kidney Ureter Urinary bladder Urethra contains water and salts.

The text explains that we know that the water kept in an earthen pot (matka) is cooler.

The text explains that 7.3 T RANSPORT OF SUBSTANCES IN P LANTS In Chapter 1 you learnt that plants take water and mineral nutrients from the soil through the roots and transport it to the leaves.

The text explains that you also learnt in Chapter 6 that food is the source of energy and every cell of an organism gets energy by the breakdown of glucose.

The text explains that transport of water and minerals Plants absorb water and minerals by the roots.

The text explains that the root hair increase the surface area of the root for the absorption of water and mineral nutrients dissolved in water.

The text explains that what kind of transport system is present in plants?

The text explains that 7.7 Transport of water and minerals in (a) a section of root, (b) a tree Root hair

The text explains that just like the red ink, minerals dissolved in water also move up the stem, along with water.

The text explains that water and minerals go to leaves and other plant parts, through narrow tubes (xylem) inside the stem (Fig.

The text explains that it also carries waste products from different parts of the body for excretion.  Circulatory system consists of the heart and blood vessels.  In humans, blood flows through arteries and veins and the heart acts as a pumping organ.  Blood consists of plasma, RBC, WBC and platelets.

The text explains that transpiration In Class VI you learnt that plants release a lot of water by the process of transpiration.

The text explains that plants absorb mineral nutrients and water from the soil.

The text explains that the water evaporates through the stomata present on the surface of the leaves by the process of transpiration.

The text explains that keywords Ammonia Artery Blood Blood vessels Capillary Circulatory system Dialysis Excretion Excretory system Haemoglobin Heart beat Kidneys Phloem Plasma Platelets Pulse Red blood cell Root hair Stethoscope Sweat Tissue Urea Ureter Urethra Uric acid Urinary bladder Vein White blood cell Xylem

The text explains that sCIENCE 80  Excretory system of humans consists of two kidneys, two ureters, a urinary bladder, and urethra.  Salts and urea are removed along with water as sweat.  Fish excrete waste substances such as ammonia which directly dissolve in water.  Birds, insects and lizard excrete uric acid in semi-solid form.  Water and mineral nutrients are absorbed by roots from the soil.  Nutrients are transported along with water to the entire plant via the vascular tissue called xylem.  The vascular tissue for the transport of food to the various parts of the plant is phloem.  A lot of water is lost by plants in the form of vapour through stomata during transpiration.  Transpiration generates a force which pulls up water absorbed by the roots from the soil, to reach the stem and leaves.

The text explains that match structures given in Column I with functions given in Column II.

The text explains that draw a diagram of the human excretory system and label the various parts.

The text explains that why is blood needed by all the parts of a body?

The text explains that why is it necessary to excrete waste products?

The text explains that extended Learning — Activities and Projects 1.

The text explains that find out about blood groups and their importance.

Source: NCERT Class 7 Science, Chapter 7: Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate

prelims_traps: Do not treat 7.1 CIRCULATORY S YSTEM and TRANSPORTATION IN ANIMALS AND PLANTS 71 as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Weather Climate and Adaptations of Animals to Climate through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 7.1 CIRCULATORY S YSTEM and TRANSPORTATION IN ANIMALS AND PLANTS 71 to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
